act: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Achterhooks.
actin: noun. A globular structural protein that polymerizes in a helical fashion to form an actin filament (or microfilament).
action: noun. The effort of performing or doing something.
ain: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Ainu.
ait: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Arikem.
ana: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Andaqui.
ani: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Andi.
anoa: noun. Either of two related species of small Indonesian water buffalo similar in appearance to deer; Bubalus quarlesi or Bubalus
ant: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Antakarinya.
anta: noun. A kind of pier that terminates the walls of the naos, a kind of post or pillar on either side of a doorway or entrance of a Greek
ava: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Avar.
avant: noun. The front of an army; the vanguard.
avian: adjective. Characteristic of or pertaining to birds, or to bird-like or flying creatures.
avo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Agavotaguerra.
can: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Chambri.
cant: noun. An argot, the jargon of a particular class or subgroup.
canto: noun. One of the chief divisions of a long poem; a book.
cat: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Catalan.
cation: noun. A positively charged ion: one that would be attracted to the cathode in electrolysis.
coat: noun. An outer garment covering the upper torso and arms.
coati: noun. Any of several omnivorous mammals, of the genus Nasua, that live in the range from the southern United States to northern Argentina.
coin: noun. A piece of currency, usually metallic and in the shape of a disc, but sometimes polygonal, or with a hole in the middle.
con: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Cofán.
icon: noun. An image, symbol, picture, portrait, or other representation, usually as an object of religious devotion.
into: preposition. To or towards the inside of.
ion: noun. An atom or group of atoms bearing an electrical charge, such as the sodium and chlorine atoms in a salt solution.
iota: noun. The ninth letter of the Greek alphabet (Ι, ι).
nit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southeastern Kolami.
not: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nomatsiguenga.
nova: noun. Any sudden brightening of a previously inconspicuous star.
oat: noun. Widely cultivated cereal grass, typically Avena sativa.
oca: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ocaina.
otic: adjective. Of, relating/pertaining to, or located near the ear.
taco: noun. A Mexican snack food made of a small tortilla (soft - or hard - shelled) filled with ingredients such as meat, rice, beans, cheese,
tao: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yami.
tav: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tatuyo.
tic: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tira.
tin: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tindi.
ton: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tongan.
tonic: adjective. Pertaining to tension, especially of the muscles.
vac: noun. Clipping of vacation (“extended period of time away from work or school”).
vacation: noun. An extended trip or journey away from home for rest or pleasure.
vain: adjective. Overly proud of oneself, especially concerning appearance; having a high opinion of one's own accomplishments with slight reason.
van: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Valman.
vat: noun. A large tub, such as is used for making wine or for tanning.
vatic: adjective. Pertaining to a prophet; prophetic, oracular.
via: noun. A main road or highway, especially in ancient Rome. (Mainly used in set phrases, below.)
vinca: noun. Any of several evergreen shrubs, of the genus Vinca, including the periwinkle.
vita: noun. A hagiography; a biography of a saint.
